---
title: Data Amount Influence
---

## Method
To understand the impact of both the number of violinists and the number of excerpts per violinist in the training data, we designed an experiment varying both parameters.  First, $20\%$ of the data was held out for testing.  Next, for systematically varied numbers of violinists ($n$) and excerpts per violinist ($m$), we trained our LTMFCC-SVM model using randomly selected subsets. The performance of the model was then measured on the fixed test set. The resulting accuracy values, for different ($n$, $m$) pairs, are presented below:

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| output: asis

import pandas as pd
import inspect
from IPython.display import Markdown, display

from violin_identification.experiments.data_scaling import (
    run_data_scaling_evaluation,
)

source_code = inspect.getsource(run_data_scaling_evaluation)

markdown_block = f"```python\n{source_code}\n```"
display(Markdown(markdown_block))

results_df = pd.read_csv("../data/interim/scaling_results.csv")
```

## Results
```{python}
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns


def plot_learning_heatmap(heatmap_df, metric="accuracy"):
    datasets = heatmap_df["dataset"].unique()
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(nrows=len(datasets), figsize=(7, 4 * len(datasets)))
    if len(datasets) == 1:
        axes = [axes]

    for ax, dataset in zip(axes, datasets):
        data = heatmap_df[heatmap_df["dataset"] == dataset]

        pivot_table = data.pivot_table(
            index="n_players",
            columns="n_excerpts_per_player",
            values=metric,
            aggfunc="mean",
        ).sort_index(ascending=False)

        sns.heatmap(
            pivot_table,
            annot=True,
            fmt=".2f",
            vmin=0,
            vmax=1.0,
            ax=ax,
        )

        ax.set_title(f"Dataset: {dataset.title()}")
        ax.set_xlabel("Excerpts per Player")
        ax.set_ylabel("Unique Players")

    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()


# --- Usage ---
plot_learning_heatmap(results_df, metric="accuracy")
```
